breakClass: .ToList()/.ToArray() (or a nested correlated-collection projection) embedded inside a server-side EF Core 10 query â€” either in a `let` clause whose value is consumed by a server `select new {...}`/`where ... .Contains(...)`, or as an inline `Field = (from ... let X=db....ToList() ... select ...).ToList()` sub-collection â€” on an outer IQueryable that has NOT yet crossed an .AsEnumerable()/.ToList() materialization boundary. EF6 client-evaluated these; EF Core 10 throws "could not be translated" (500). Outer queries that are materialized first (var data2 = v.ToList(); from o in data2 ...) or sourced from db.X.AsEnumerable() are SAFE.
why: Source from db.HireReturns has NO .AsEnumerable()/.ToList() boundary before the let bindings; the whole select new (incl. app/AppStatus/chkAppStatus) is materialized server-side at line 232 .ToList(). The let ...ToList() collection projections â€” especially chkAppStatus's GroupBy(...).Select(g=>...FirstOrDefault()).ToList().Select(...).ToList() â€” cannot be translated by EF Core 10. Legacy HireReturnController.cs:179 has the identical pattern and worked in EF6 -> port regression. NOTE: the parallel MyReports endpoints were fixed by adding .AsEnumerable() to the source; this register-grid endpoint was missed.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED â€” the hunter's claim holds. File: QuickNetCore/Controllers/HireReturnController.cs, method GetHireReturn, lines 185-189 (let bindings) materialized at line 232 (.ToList()).

(a) SERVER-SIDE â€” verified. Source is `from b in db.HireReturns join ...` (line 170). I traced every line from 170 to 232: there is NO .ToList()/.ToArray()/.AsEnumerable()/.AsAsyncEnumerable() boundary anywhere before the `select new {...}`. The first materialization is the `.ToList()` at line 232. Confirmed via Grep that the only .AsEnumerable() calls in this file are in OTHER methods (lines 377, 1853, 1890, 2271) â€” GetHireReturn has none. So the `let app/AppStatus/chkAppStatus` (lines 185-189) feed a server-translated projection (app=app, AppStatus=AppStatus, chkAppStatus=chkAppStatus at lines 226-228). All referenced sets are real DbSets (Approvals, ApprovalUpdates verified in ApplicationDbContext.cs lines 354-355), so these are correlated server subqueries, not client locals. Not a group-aggregate over a non-empty IGrouping either â€” see below.

(b) WOULD 500 â€” verified, severity HIGH. The decisive offender is `chkAppStatus` (lines 187-189): db.ApprovalUpdates.Where(...).GroupBy(l => l.ApprovedBy).Select(g => g.OrderByDescending(c => c.CreatedDate).FirstOrDefault()).ToList().Select(x => x.ApprovalStatus).ToList(). The GroupBy element selector projects a WHOLE ENTITY per group (g.OrderByDescending(...).FirstOrDefault() returns an ApprovalUpdate row, not an aggregate). EF Core's relational translator cannot translate a GroupBy whose selector returns non-aggregated rows â€” it throws InvalidOperationException "The LINQ expression ... could not be translated." Embedded as a correlated collection in the outer unmaterialized projection, this surfaces as an HTTP 500 at line 232 enumeration. Translation fails BEFORE row evaluation, so it 500s on the FIRST request regardless of data (even empty ApprovalUpdates) â€” hence high, not edge-only. (The trailing .ToList().Select(...).ToList() does NOT create a client boundary because the whole expression is still inside the outer IQueryable's expression tree.) The `app` and `AppStatus` scalar collection subqueries are translatable on their own; chkAppStatus is the guaranteed breaker.

PORT REGRESSION â€” verified. Legacy _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/HireReturnController.cs lines 164-252 is structurally byte-identical (same source, same three let .ToList() bindings, same GroupBy().Select(g=>...FirstOrDefault()), same .ToList() at line 224). EF6 client-evaluated the unsupported final projection (LINQ-to-Objects fallback); EF Core 10 has no automatic client-eval in a server query's final projection â†’ regression. The parallel MyReports endpoints and this file's own SearchInvoiceItem (line 2271) were already fixed with .AsEnumerable(); this register grid was missed, exactly as claimed.

method: HireReturn index/grid action (from b in db.HireReturns)
breakClass: GroupBy-then-Select(g => g.OrderBy*/FirstOrDefault()) returning an ENTITY/row, used server-side (JOINed on g.FirstOrDefault(), or composed as a correlated 'latest-per-key' subquery in a non-materialized IQueryable). EF Core 10 cannot translate the grouped FirstOrDefault outside an aggregate context -> "could not be translated" runtime 500. Every finding has the identical shape in _archive/legacy-mvc5 where EF6 translated it, so each is a port regression.
why: Outer query 'from b in db.HireReturns' has NO AsEnumerable()/ToList() before the let, so chkAppStatus is a correlated subquery composed into the server-side projection (boundary = trailing .ToList() after select, line 232). chkAppStatus = GroupBy(ApprovedBy).Select(g => g.OrderByDescending(CreatedDate).FirstOrDefault()) filtered by b.HireReturnId -> correlated 'latest ApprovalUpdate per approver' returning ENTITIES. EF Core 10 cannot translate this nested GroupBy+FirstOrDefault correlated subquery -> 500 on the HireReturn register grid. Already-fixed siblings (operationprocedure:597/601, MyReports:10552+) use .AsEnumerable() before .GroupBy, proving the remedy. Legacy has the identical let => port regression.
finalFix:
In Controllers/HireReturnController.cs, method GetHireReturn, change the chkAppStatus let (lines 187-189) by inserting .AsEnumerable() immediately before .GroupBy(l => l.ApprovedBy), forcing the grouped-FirstOrDefault to evaluate client-side per row (mirrors legacy EF6 behavior, the MyReportsController.cs:10660 fix, and operationprocedureController.cs:601).

FROM:
                     let chkAppStatus = db.ApprovalUpdates.Where(x => x.TransEntry == b.HireReturnId && x.Type == "HireReturn").GroupBy(l => l.ApprovedBy)
                                        .Select(g => g.OrderByDescending(c => c.CreatedDate).FirstOrDefault())
                                        .ToList().Select(x => x.ApprovalStatus).ToList()

TO:
                     let chkAppStatus = db.ApprovalUpdates.Where(x => x.TransEntry == b.HireReturnId && x.Type == "HireReturn").AsEnumerable().GroupBy(l => l.ApprovedBy)
                                        .Select(g => g.OrderByDescending(c => c.CreatedDate).FirstOrDefault())
                                        .Select(x => x.ApprovalStatus).ToList()

The redundant intermediate .ToList() (before .Select(x => x.ApprovalStatus)) can be dropped since AsEnumerable already moves evaluation client-side, but keeping it is harmless and also correct. chkAppStatus remains a List<ApprovalStatus>, preserving all downstream usage at line 256 (o.chkAppStatus.Count > 0, .Contains(ApprovalStatus.Rejected), .Count() == o.app.Count()). Output is bit-for-bit identical to the legacy EF6 result.
reasoning:
I independently inspected Controllers/HireReturnController.cs (method GetHireReturn, the HireReturn register-grid data action) and confirmed the claim.

SERVER-SIDE: The outer query at line 170 (from b in db.HireReturns join a in db.Customers ... join ... select new {...}) has NO .AsEnumerable()/.ToList()/.AsAsyncEnumerable() boundary before its projection. The only materialization boundary is the trailing .ToList() at line 232 (}).ToList().Select(o => new {...})). Therefore the entire select-new projection â€” including the chkAppStatus let (lines 187-189) â€” is composed into a single server-side IQueryable that EF Core 10 must translate to SQL. chkAppStatus is correlated on b.HireReturnId, so it is a correlated subquery inside that SQL projection, not a standalone top-level query. The inner .ToList() at line 189 (...FirstOrDefault()).ToList().Select(x => x.ApprovalStatus).ToList()) does NOT save it: inside a non-materialized server-side projection, a collection-producing let becomes a correlated collection subquery whose body must still be translated to SQL; .ToList() only marks where the returned collection is materialized client-side AFTER SQL executes.

WHY IT 500s: The subquery body is db.ApprovalUpdates.Where(correlated on b.HireReturnId & Type=="HireReturn").GroupBy(l => l.ApprovedBy).Select(g => g.OrderByDescending(c => c.CreatedDate).FirstOrDefault()). The GroupBy result selector returns a full ENTITY row (ApprovalUpdate) via FirstOrDefault(), not a scalar aggregate. This is the canonical EF Core 10 untranslatable shape: a grouped FirstOrDefault returning a row outside an aggregate context, embedded in a non-materialized query. EF Core 10 throws "could not be translated" at runtime -> 500. This fires every time the action runs (the subquery is always composed), so it is high severity, not edge-only. ApprovalUpdate entity (Models/Approval.cs) confirmed to have ApprovedBy(string), CreatedDate, ApprovalStatus, so the fix preserves output.

PORT REGRESSION: _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/HireReturnController.cs lines 181-183 are byte-identical (no AsEnumerable); EF6 translated it. So this is a genuine port regression.

REMEDY PROVEN BY SIBLINGS: MyReportsController.cs:10660 handles the IDENTICAL HireReturn/ApprovalUpdate case and was already fixed by inserting .AsEnumerable() before .GroupBy(l => l.ApprovedBy); operationprocedureController.cs:601 and ~18 other MyReports lines use the same remedy. (Note: the GroupBy-FirstOrDefault instances in Models/Common.cs lines 16218/16234/16252 are SAFE â€” standalone helper-method locals materialized by their own .ToList(), client-side â€” not relevant here.)

The hunter's analysis is correct on every load-bearing point. Confirmed.
